name: High Number of Service Stop or Task Kill in Short Span
description: Detects a high number of service stop or task kill commands within a short timeframe, which can indicate malicious activity such as attempts to disable security tools or disrupt system operations.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of the last detected service stop or task kill command.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account that initiated the commands.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The hostname where the event occurred.

parameters.name: process_name
parameters.description: The name of the process that executed the commands (e.g., 'taskkill.exe', 'sc.exe', 'net.exe').

parameters.name: parent_process
parameters.description: The parent process of the command.

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The full command line used, including arguments for stopping services or killing tasks.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: The total number of service stop or task kill commands detected within the timeframe.
